question_title: What will be the value of "CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index" on 2026-04-09?
summary: The CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index (GVZCLS) is currently at 42.71 (March 30, 2026), in a confirmed crisis regime with a FRED-detected regime mean of 44.43. The elevated level is driven by the 2026 Iran war (ongoing since Feb 28, with Houthi escalation on Mar 28), tariff uncertainty, and a massive 17.4% gold price drawdown in March. Over the 7-trading-day horizon to April 9, I model three scenarios: crisis persistence at current regime (70%), de-escalation toward 35 (20%), and further escalation toward 52 (10%), using an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reverting process with empirically calibrated daily volatility of 8-10%. The median forecast is ~42.5 (approximately the current level), with significant right-skew reflecting the possibility of further crisis spikes. The distribution is wide (P10=32, P90=56) due to the extremely high daily volatility of GVZ itself (8-10% daily log returns).

factors.description: Current value of GVZ is 42.71 (Mar 30). FRED regime analysis shows new regime mean at 44.43 since Mar 26 (std 1.23, 3 observations).
Iran war ongoing since Feb 28 with Houthi escalation on Mar 28, Strait of Hormuz closed. Sustains elevated gold volatility regime. No ceasefire expected within 7 trading days.
GVZ daily log-return volatility of 0.08-0.10 (extremely high) means wide uncertainty over 7 trading days. Random walk 1-sigma range: 33-54.
VIX-type indices are mean-reverting. Current value 42.71 is slightly below regime mean 44.43, suggesting mild upward pull toward regime center.
Possibility of de-escalation if diplomatic talks progress (Trump mentioned 'postponing attacks for talks' on Mar 23). Would push GVZ back toward 30-35.
Gold experienced massive 17.4% drawdown in March. If selling exhaustion occurs, realized vol could decline, pulling GVZ down. VIX-type indices historically decay rapidly even during crises.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary: CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index on 2026-04-09

**Median forecast: 42.5** (approximately current level of 42.71)

### Key Distribution Points
| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P5 | 29.80 |
| P10 | 32.29 |
| P25 | 36.86 |
| P50 | **42.50** |
| P75 | 48.97 |
| P90 | 55.92 |
| P95 | 60.81 |

### Rationale
The GVZ is in an extreme crisis regime driven by the Iran war, Houthi escalation, and ongoing tariff uncertainty. FRED regime analysis confirms a structural shift since March 26 with a regime mean of 44.43. My Monte Carlo simulation models three scenarios:

1. **Crisis persists (70%)** — GVZ oscillates around regime mean ~44
2. **De-escalation (20%)** — diplomatic progress pulls GVZ back toward ~35
3. **Further escalation (10%)** — new military developments push GVZ above 50

The distribution is wide (80% CI: 32-56) due to the extraordinarily high daily volatility of the index itself (~8-10% daily), and right-skewed reflecting the potential for crisis spikes. The median stays near the current value, reflecting the dominant expectation that the crisis regime persists through the 7-trading-day horizon.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index" on 2026-04-09?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42984)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series GVZCLS once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=GVZCLS`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-09 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.

factors.description: Current value of GVZ is 42.71 (Mar 30), providing the starting point. FRED regime analysis shows new regime mean at 44.43 since Mar 26.
Iran war ongoing since Feb 28 with Houthi escalation on Mar 28, Strait of Hormuz closed. This sustains elevated gold volatility regime. No ceasefire expected within 7 trading days.
GVZ daily log-return volatility of 0.08-0.10 (extremely high) means wide uncertainty over 7 trading days. Random walk 1-sigma range: 33-54.
VIX-type indices are mean-reverting. Current value 42.71 is slightly below regime mean 44.43, suggesting mild upward pull.
Possibility of de-escalation if diplomatic talks progress (Trump mentioned 'postponing attacks for talks' on Mar 23). Would push GVZ back toward 30-35.
Gold experienced massive 17.4% drawdown in March. If selling exhaustion occurs, realized vol could decline, pulling GVZ down.

input.anchor: Current value of 42.71 with FRED regime mean at 44.43 (since Mar 26, std 1.23). Median of Monte Carlo simulation: 42.50.
input.assessment: The CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index (GVZ) is currently in an extraordinarily elevated crisis regime at 42.71, driven by the convergence of the 2026 Iran war (beginning Feb 28), ongoing tariff uncertainty, and a massive gold price drawdown (~17.4% peak-to-trough in March). FRED regime analysis confirms a structural upward shift since March 26 with a stable regime mean of 44.43. Over the 7-trading-day horizon to April 9, the dominant scenario is persistence of the crisis regime, with the GVZ oscillating around the regime mean. The daily volatility of GVZ itself is extremely high (8-10% daily log returns), creating wide uncertainty. My Monte Carlo simulation uses a scenario mixture: 70% crisis persists (OU toward 44.43), 20% de-escalation (OU toward 35), 10% further escalation (OU toward 52), with a volatility blend to account for parameter uncertainty. The median output of ~42.5 is essentially the current value, reflecting that the crisis regime is expected to persist but is balanced by mild mean-reversion forces.
input.calibration_notes: The 80% CI (P10-P90) spans 32.29 to 55.92, a width of ~24 index points. This is roughly 1.5x the random-walk 1-sigma range of ~18 points, which is appropriate for a scenario mixture that includes both de-escalation and escalation. The distribution is right-skewed, which is correct for VIX-type indices. The upper tail extends to ~72 at P99, which is plausible for crisis-period GVZ spikes (the all-time high during COVID was ~50-60, but the current geopolitical crisis is more severe).
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether the Iran war de-escalates (ceasefire, diplomatic breakthrough) - this would sharply reduce GVZ. 2. Whether the Houthi involvement expands to disrupt additional shipping routes - this would spike GVZ further. 3. Whether gold selling exhaustion occurs, reducing realized vol. 4. Parameter uncertainty in daily GVZ vol (0.08 vs 0.10 is a big difference over 7 days).
input.tool_audit: FRED fetch_url: returned complete daily data for March with regime stats - very useful. research() sub-agent: returned comprehensive data on GVZ levels, gold market context, Iran war impact, tariff situation, and prediction markets - extremely helpful. Monte Carlo simulation in sandbox: executed correctly for all scenarios. No tool failures.
input.update_triggers: 1. Iran ceasefire announcement → GVZ drops to 30-35 range. 2. New military escalation (e.g., Israeli/US strikes expanding beyond Iran) → GVZ spikes above 50. 3. Major gold market event (central bank intervention, margin requirement changes) → large GVZ move in either direction.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was very effective at gathering comprehensive context on the gold market situation. The FRED API integration in fetch_url is excellent - providing both raw data and regime statistics automatically. The Monte Carlo simulation approach worked well for this type of question. One friction point: I had to manually check the trading calendar (Good Friday) - a built-in trading day calculator would be helpful. The scenario mixture approach feels appropriate for this crisis-regime forecasting situation.
